headlines.title: China's Coal Habit Is Outpacing Its Clean Energy Boom
headlines.summary: A recent social media post from a meteorologist with a large following made a claim that deserves a closer look. headlines.summary: Abu Dhabi’s XRG, the international investment arm of the Abu Dhabi National Oil Company (ADNOC), and Italy’s Eni S.p.A. headlines.title: ING: Oil Prices Have Overshot To The Downside
headlines.summary: Global oil prices have collapsed back to pre-war levels, trading down near $70 per barrel, with a potential peace deal between the U.S. and Iran helping calm global markets and prompting the removal of massive war risk premiums from energy valuations. headlines.title: Is Libya Quietly Becoming the Biggest Oil Prize the West Can’t Afford to Ignore?

headlines.title: Russia's Fuel Crisis Deepens as Putin Acknowledges Growing Gasoline Shortages
headlines.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin acknowledged what outside experts, many regional officials, and everyday citizens have already noticed: Russia is facing energy shortages, causing major problems for citizens and industries. headlines.title: Pakistan Plans to Boost LPG Imports and Mulls Cheaper Oil Supply from Iran
headlines.summary: Pakistan will raise its imports of liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) from Iran and considers the idea of importing cheaper crude oil from the Islamic Republic, Pakistani Minister of Petroleum, Ali Pervaiz Malik, has said. headlines.title: Gulf Producers Race to Load Oil and LNG as Hormuz Stays Open
headlines.summary: Saudi Arabia, the United Arab Emirates, and Qatar continue loading oil and LNG onto vessels at their Persian Gulf ports despite the attacks on two commercial vessels near the Strait of Hormuz and the weekend tensions between the U.S. and Iran. headlines.title: Pakistan LNG Seeks Emergency Cargo After Weekend Hormuz Flare-Up
headlines.summary: Pakistan’s state LNG importer is urgently seeking to procure an LNG cargo for delivery this week, with offers due on June 29, as traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remains volatile amid persistent threats to tankers and reignited U.S.-Iran tensions.
